rationale.R1.1: The section contains an explicit contribution claim ('first per-commit routing benchmark') and an implicit gap statement (prior work assumes all analyzers are always run). Reviewing the section centrally includes verifying that the thesis is coherently constructed and the gap is well-motivated.
rationale.R1.2: There is a marked epistemic inconsistency across the section: paragraph 2 is saturated with extreme hedges ('tentatively surveyed,' 'broadly speaking,' 'It seems reasonable to suppose,' 'might, in principle,' 'presumably') while paragraph 4 issues confident first-person claims. This cross-section incoherence makes logical consistency a central review responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: The section asserts specific quantitative outcomes (78% union-recall, 31% compute cost, 11,400 commits, 47 repositories, four named analyzers). Verifying internal consistency and checking that these numbers are coherently anchored to the described experimental design is a central review task.
rationale.R1.4: The paper's novelty is staked on a strong priority claim ('the first per-commit routing benchmark'). Assessing whether the related-work paragraph adequately substantiates that claim — and whether the described prior work genuinely lacks this capability — is central to any substantive review.

parsed.clarification_question: Is the intended review a substantive/technical review (checking argument structure, evidence alignment, and novelty claims) or a writing/prose review (checking sentence quality, clarity, and rhetorical consistency)?
